title: lonicera iberica f. microphylla
intro: Lonicera iberica f. microphylla, also known as dwarf Honeysuckle, is a flowering plant species in the family Caprifoliaceae, native to the Caucasus region. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its berries, leaves, and stems, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Lonicera iberica f. microphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or extract, and is also used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
